figures.caption: Figure 3: Top: Online bias estimate b̂t (solid red) tracking the true running mean (dashed blue) on mean-shift data (Ridge, h = 1). The grey band shows the dead-zone |b̂t| ≤ k ·MAD. Pre-shift, b̂t stays inside the dead-zone; post-shift (t > 1000), it rapidly converges to b ≈ 3.99. Bottom: Raw residuals showing the shift in location.

Figure 2: Winkler score ratios (BC-ACI / ACI) across all datasets. Values below 1 (red bars, left) indicate BC-ACI improvement. Grey bars (right) are real datasets with no known distribution shift in the evaluation window.

Table 1: Main results: BC-ACI vs. ACI (688 runs). Winkler ratio < 1 means BC-ACI is better. p-values from paired one-sided Wilcoxon signed-rank tests. Bold = statistically significant improvement (p < 0.001). The adaptive dead-zone threshold k = 0.5 is used throughout.

Figure 1: Prediction intervals around a mean shift at t = 1000 (Ridge model, h = 1). Top: Standard ACI widens symmetrically to maintain coverage. Bottom: BC-ACI detects the bias, shifts the interval centre, and achieves tighter intervals post-shift while preserving coverage.
